title: China-Italy Double Tax Treaty 2026: FAQs for Italian Investors - China Briefing
summary: China-Italy Double Tax Treaty 2026: FAQs for Italian Investors China Briefing
ai_summary: This item provides FAQs for Italian investors regarding the China-Italy Double Tax Treaty, which is expected to be effective from 2026. It offers practical insights into the application of the bilateral treaty, addressing common international tax considerations for cross-border investments between China and Italy.

title: Parliament ratifies double taxation avoidance agreement with Japan - AKIpress News Agency
summary: Parliament ratifies double taxation avoidance agreement with Japan AKIpress News Agency
ai_summary: An unnamed parliament, likely in Central Asia based on the news source, has ratified a Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA) with Japan. This ratification marks a crucial domestic step for the DTAA's entry into force, aiming to facilitate bilateral trade and investment by preventing double taxation.

title: Ukraine and Germany have signed a new agreement on the avoidance of double taxation - Odessa Journal
summary: Ukraine and Germany have signed a new agreement on the avoidance of double taxation Odessa Journal
ai_summary: Ukraine and Germany have officially signed a new Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement (DTAA), replacing an older treaty to modernize tax cooperation. This new agreement is intended to provide greater tax certainty for investors and businesses operating between the two countries, critically supporting Ukraine's economic recovery and reconstruction efforts.

title: California advances game preservation bill as digital tax debate grows - MSN
summary: California advances game preservation bill as digital tax debate grows MSN
ai_summary: California is progressing with a game preservation bill amidst an escalating debate over digital taxation at the state level. This discussion underscores the ongoing challenges for jurisdictions like California in adapting existing tax frameworks to effectively tax digital products and services.

title: Nigerian Govt Launches Free Digital Tax Dispute Platforms to Support Businesses and Taxpayers - MSME Africa
summary: Nigerian Govt Launches Free Digital Tax Dispute Platforms to Support Businesses and Taxpayers MSME Africa
ai_summary: The Nigerian government has introduced free digital platforms dedicated to resolving tax disputes for businesses and taxpayers. This initiative aims to enhance the accessibility and efficiency of tax dispute resolution processes, potentially benefitting foreign investors and local enterprises operating in Nigeria.
